title: synth_intel_alm: replacement flow for ALM-based Intel FPGAs.
body:
`synth_intel` has long been neglected, and I think it should be divided into two back-end sub-scripts to better represent architectural differences between LUT4+FF ("Logic Element") based FPGAs, such as the Cyclone IV and Cyclone 10LP; and LUT6+4xFF ("Adapatable Logic Module") based FPGAs, such as the Stratix II, Cyclone V and Cyclone 10GX. For now, this covers the ALM family; specifically the Cyclone V. 

The vendor LUT primitives (`{stratixii,cyclonev,cyclone10gx}_lcell_comb`) for the ALM architectures have been replaced with Yosys-internal `MISTRAL_ALMk` primitives; this allows Intel passes to operate on a single primitive family instead of multiple, deferring vendor primitive mapping until the end.

The equivalency tests have been ported from Efinix, barring `memory.ys` (I don't currently have any RAM models) and `latches.ys` (I don't have any latch models).

This is missing a lot of things such as DSP mapping, RAM initialisation, but it's a start.
